name: Flat Earth Society
description: The Flat Earth Society is a modern organization that promotes the pseudoscientific belief that the Earth is flat rather than a sphere (geoid). This belief contradicts centuries of scientific observation and evidence. Proponents argue that various institutions, including governments, scientists, and airlines, are engaged in a massive conspiracy to conceal the 'truth' of a flat Earth. The modern Flat Earth movement gained traction in the mid-20th century with the revival of Samuel Shenton's Universal Zetetic Society, later evolving into the Flat Earth Society. Key figures include Samuel Shenton, Charles K. Johnson, and more recently, individuals who have used the internet and social media to spread their views. The 'evidence' cited by flat-Earthers includes misinterpretations of gravity, perspective, and celestial observations. They often point to the lack of visible curvature in everyday landscapes and challenge established scientific models with alternative explanations, often relying on selective evidence and logical fallacies. The mainstream scientific perspective firmly rejects the flat Earth theory, citing overwhelming evidence from direct observation, satellite imagery, GPS technology, circumnavigation, lunar eclipses, and countless other scientific experiments and observations. The theory is considered a conspiracy theory due to its reliance on the assumption of widespread deception and its rejection of established scientific consensus.
subtitle: Belief in a non-spherical Earth

claims.claim: The Earth is a flat disc.
claims.evidence: Lack of perceived curvature on the horizon, water always finds its level, airplane flights seeming to take unnatural routes.
claims.criticism: The Earth's curvature is subtle but measurable, particularly over long distances. Flight paths appear curved on flat maps due to map projections. Water 'finding its level' only demonstrates local level, not global flatness.

claims.claim: Gravity is not a force; objects are simply less dense than the surrounding medium and rise or fall accordingly (buoyancy).
claims.evidence: Alternative interpretations of experiments involving falling objects, questioning the standard model of physics.
claims.criticism: This contradicts well-established physics, including Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation and Einstein's theory of General Relativity. These theories have been repeatedly verified through experiments and observations.

claims.claim: NASA and other space agencies are faking space travel and satellite imagery.
claims.evidence: Perceived inconsistencies in NASA footage, questioning the feasibility of space travel, alleging manipulated images and videos.
claims.criticism: This requires a massive conspiracy involving thousands of individuals across multiple countries. Independent verification of satellite data and GPS technology exists. The cost and complexity of faking all space-related activities are immense and unsustainable.

keyFigures.name: Samuel Shenton
keyFigures.role: Revived the Flat Earth Society in the mid-20th century.

keyFigures.name: Charles K. Johnson
keyFigures.role: President of the Flat Earth Society after Shenton.

keyFigures.name: Neil deGrasse Tyson
keyFigures.role: Astrophysicist and prominent critic of the Flat Earth theory.

mythologyConnections: Ancient cosmologies often depicted the Earth as flat, with a dome-like sky.
Some interpretations of religious texts are used to support a flat Earth.

timeline.event: Many ancient cultures believed in a flat Earth.
Eratosthenes accurately calculated the Earth's circumference using geometry.
Samuel Shenton revives the Flat Earth Society.
The Flat Earth Society gains popularity online.

sources.author: Christine Garwood
sources.work: Flat Earth: The History of an Infamous Idea
sources.title: Christine Garwood
